definition: A digital representation of a person powered by AI, often combining voice cloning and video synthesis to create realistic virtual presenters. Used in content creation to maintain consistent delivery.

When a social platform algorithmically reduces the reach of posts detected as AI-generated. LinkedIn's 2026 crackdown suppresses AI-written posts in the recommendation feed while simultaneously expanding its own AI writing tools — creating a strategic paradox for B2B content teams.

An AI-driven product discovery feature where social platforms use creator content to surface relevant products to users. Meta's Muse Spark Shopping Mode on Instagram creates a loop where AI matches creator posts with shoppable products — changing how products get discovered on social media.

AI systems that can autonomously take actions, make decisions, and complete multi-step tasks without human intervention at each step. Moves beyond chat-based assistants to agents that browse, code, and execute workflows.
The average cost to acquire one customer through advertising. A key metric for measuring ad campaign efficiency. Lower CPA means more efficient ad spend.
The cost an advertiser pays per 1,000 ad impressions. Standard podcast advertising metric, typically $25-50 for business/tech podcasts.

Legal action taken against AI companies for unauthorized use of copyrighted content in training data or generated outputs. Increasingly common as media companies challenge AI video and image generators.

The ability to maintain a character's appearance, proportions, and visual identity across multiple AI-generated video scenes. Previously the hardest unsolved problem in AI video — tools like Higgsfield Soul Cast now address this with parametric design systems.

The expense of running AI models on GPU infrastructure. Sora's shutdown illustrated this dramatically — generating $2.1M in revenue while burning $15M per day in compute costs, making the product economically unviable despite strong user interest.

The maximum amount of text an AI model can process in a single conversation, measured in tokens. GPT-5.4's 1M token context window means it can analyze roughly 750,000 words at once — equivalent to several full-length books.

A server-to-server connection between your website and an ad platform (like Meta) that tracks customer actions more accurately than browser-based pixels. Meta's one-click setup eliminated the typical $500-$2,000 developer cost for SMBs.
Chinese AI lab offering frontier-level AI models at roughly 90% less cost than Western competitors. Disrupted the market by proving high-quality AI doesn't require massive budgets.

The AI architecture behind most image and video generators. Works by learning to remove noise from random static, gradually revealing coherent images. Powers tools like DALL-E, Midjourney, and Runway.

Technology that stitches audio advertisements into podcast episodes in real-time as they're downloaded or streamed, allowing different listeners to hear different ads and enabling ads to be updated after publication.

An AI capability where a single session spawns hundreds of parallel agents to complete complex tasks simultaneously. Claude Opus 4.8's Dynamic Workflows let content teams run research, drafting, and optimization in parallel rather than sequentially.

The process of taking a pre-trained AI model and further training it on specialized data to improve performance for specific tasks. Like teaching a general-purpose assistant to become an expert in your industry.
The most capable and advanced AI models available at any given time. Currently includes GPT-5.x, Claude Opus, and Gemini 3.x. These push the boundaries of what AI can accomplish.
The core AI technology behind chatbots like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. Trained on vast text data to understand and generate human-like language. The foundation of most modern AI tools.

The ability to edit specific regions within a video frame — changing a background, swapping an object, or adjusting a detail — without regenerating the entire clip. Runway's Aleph 2.0 Edit Studio brings this Photoshop-style control to AI video for the first time.

The practice of testing the same prompt across multiple AI models to compare quality, speed, and cost before committing to one for production. Adobe Firefly's multi-model hub makes this possible inside a single workflow — run the same brief through Kling, Runway, Veo, and Ray side by side.

A standard that lets AI tools connect to external services with a single authentication step. Runway's MCP integration means you can generate video inside Claude or ChatGPT without leaving the chat — one OAuth connection replaces API keys and platform switching.

A platform that integrates multiple AI models from different providers into a single interface. Runway's addition of Seedance 2.0 and Nano Banana Pro alongside its own Gen-4.5 lets creators choose the best model for each video task without switching tools.

An approach where a single AI system coordinates multiple frontier models (like Claude, GPT, Gemini, Grok) simultaneously, routing each subtask to the best-suited model. Perplexity Computer uses this to complete entire projects from a single prompt.
AI models that can process and generate multiple types of content — text, images, audio, and video — within a single system. The trend driving convergence of AI tools.

An AI model released with an open license (like Apache 2.0) that allows anyone to download, modify, and deploy it freely. Google's Gemma 4 runs locally on your laptop with no API fees or data leaving your machine — a cost-elimination play for SMB teams.
The skill of crafting effective instructions for AI models to get optimal outputs. Involves techniques like providing context, examples, and specific formatting requirements.

An AI workflow where a text description generates a functional design prototype — pitch decks, landing pages, or marketing visuals — without manual design tools. Claude Design's launch disrupted the design tool market enough to drop Figma's stock 7%.
A technique where AI models pull relevant information from a knowledge base before generating responses. Reduces hallucinations and keeps AI answers grounded in real, current data.

Restrictions on how many AI requests you can make per time period. Anthropic doubled Claude's rate limits overnight via a SpaceX compute deal — eliminating mid-workflow throttling that previously interrupted content production for Pro and Max users.

A registration statement filed with the SEC before a company goes public (IPO). Anthropic's confidential S-1 draft signals a near-trillion-dollar public offering — important for SMBs standardizing on Claude, as it means long-term platform commitment and stability.

An AI workflow where you provide a script or outline and the tool automatically generates a complete video with scenes, transitions, and visuals. CapCut's rebuilt AI Video Maker brings this to zero cost, cutting production time to 15 minutes.

An AI technique that applies the visual style of one image (or brand moodboard) to newly generated content. Krea 2's style transfer system lets you upload existing campaign assets and generate new images that maintain brand consistency across all outputs.
AI technology that generates video clips from written descriptions. Rapidly evolving space with tools like Runway, Kling, Sora, and Seedance competing for the top spot.

An AI-powered conversational system that speaks and listens in real-time, handling tasks like customer support, FAQ responses, and appointment booking. ElevenLabs' 50+ templates make these accessible to SMBs without custom development.
AI technology that replicates a specific person's voice from audio samples, enabling synthetic speech that sounds like the original speaker. Used in podcasting, dubbing, and accessibility.

AI agents that automate multi-step workflows within a productivity platform. OpenAI's Workspace Agents can handle content research, drafting, and publishing sequences autonomously — running overnight while you sleep.
